perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Upon request, the Receiving Party agrees to promptly return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Con fidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party and thereafter the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall be determined, save for the Receiving Party being liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Upon request, the Receiving Party may choose to return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Confidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party is not responsible to return the copy of the documents.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the prompt return or destruction of Confidential Information upon request. The modified text makes this return/destruction optional ('may choose') and removes the responsibility of returnig copies. The contradictory is creating an uncertianty about the responsibility of returning the documents. This directly conflicts with Section 4, regarding the term of the agreement, as it creates uncertainty about what happens to the information after the agreement ends, if the destruction of the copies is now optinal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate (i) upon the completion of the Project (if the Receiving Party is selected for the Project) ; or (ii) on the date falling three (3 ) years from the date of this Agreement or such other date as the parties may mutually agree in writing (if the Receiving Party is not selected for the Project .
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information, whereas the changed text makes this optional and removes the responsibility of returning copies. This directly contradicts section 4.2, which outlines the termination of the agreement and the obligations of the parties. By making the return or destruction of information optional, the changed text creates uncertainty about what happens to the information after the agreement terminates, directly conflicting with the implied expectation in section 4.2 that confidential information will be handled appropriately upon termination, which is a structural flaw in the text as defined by the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate (i) upon the completion of the Project (if the Receiving Party is selected for the Project) ; or (ii) on the date falling three (3 ) years from the date of this Agreement or such other date as the parties may mutually agree in writing (if the Receiving Party is not selected for the Project .
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall not terminate under any circumstances; this Agreement will stay in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4.2 originally specifies clear termination conditions. The modified version states that the agreement *never* terminates. This directly contradicts the original termination clauses and introduces an in-text contradiction that is contradictory. This is creating an uncertianty in time, since now the contract is valid forever. The main place where the contradiction takes place in Section 4.3, since that section refers to breaches durign the agreement, but if the agreement doesn't end, how can there be a breach durign or after the agreement?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Notwithstanding the termination pursuant to Clause 4.2 above the Receiving Party shall remain liable for all antecedent breaches duri ng the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.2 states the agreement 'shall not terminate under any circumstances', which directly contradicts the contradicted text in Section 4.3 that refers to 'termination pursuant to Clause 4.2' and 'antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement'. The original text in 4.2 specified termination conditions, while the changed text eliminates them entirely, creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.9 The Disclosing Party shall not have any liability or responsibility for errors or omissions in, or any business decisions made by the Receiving Party in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.9 The Disclosing Party shall be fully liable and responsible for all errors or omissions in, or any business decisions made by the Receiving Party in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 5.9 explicitly states that the Disclosing Party has no liability for errors, omissions, or business decisions based on the Confidential Information. By inverting this to state that the Disclosing Party *is* fully liable, a direct contradiction is created. It creates uncertainty regarding where the liability rests. The contradicted location is Section 6.1, due to the fact that the section refers to any disputes arising out or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Malaysia and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of Malaysia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text inverts the original section 5.9, shifting liability from none to full. While section 6.1 governs dispute resolution regarding the agreement, the altered section 5.9 fundamentally changes the liabilities under the agreement, creating a direct contradiction regarding who bears responsibility for errors, omissions, or business decisions. This alteration directly contradicts the implicit assumption of the original contract structure where the Disclosing Party was not liable, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction and therefore, according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Malaysia and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of Malaysia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Singapore and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of Singapore shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts. However the courts of Malaysia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the laws and courts of Malaysia have jurisdiction. The modification adds that the laws and courts of Singapore have jurisdiction. It also states that Malaysia also has exclusive jurisdiction. Now there are 2 countries that have exclusive jurisdiction. This contradiction regarding which country's legal system governs the agreement introduces uncertainty about which laws and courts will be used to resolve disputes. This can lead to different legal interpretations. The in-text contradiction is in the last sentence, where it contradict itself by refering that Malaysia has jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of Singapore and each of the parties irrevocably agrees that the courts of Singapore shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and that accordingly any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement may be brought in such courts. However the courts of Malaysia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws and courts of Singapore, but then it also states that the courts of Malaysia have exclusive jurisdiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which now contains a contradiction by stating that both Singapore and Malaysia have exclusive jurisdiction, thus satisfying the conditions for Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as it places conflicting implications in the same section.
